description: Operational configuration contract fixtures for deterministic policy checks, not model-output or runtime tests.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved GA4 newsletter_subscription requirement in an authorized dedicated workspace. The plan contains exactly method and form_location, and the source contract and representative payload are complete. Runtime recette and publication are outside this request.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.method
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved source field.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.form_location
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved source field.

expected_actions.name: CE - newsletter_subscription
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved source event.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - newsletter_subscription
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved analytics event and two-field payload.

external_dependencies: Publication and runtime recette remain separate workflows.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an explicit Meta Purchase media brief in an authorized dedicated workspace. Pixel identity, catalog SKU convention, value, currency, transaction timing, two-item payload, installed template, and strict CMP grant are supplied. Runtime recette and publication are outside this request.

expected_actions.name: Meta - Base - Pixel
expected_actions.justification: Existing initialization is semantically compatible.

expected_actions.name: CJS - Meta contents
expected_actions.justification: Required narrow array reshape preserves both approved items.

expected_actions.name: CJS - Meta content_ids
expected_actions.justification: Required narrow identifier array uses the approved catalog SKU.

expected_actions.name: CE - purchase - Meta valid
expected_actions.justification: Normal event plus required ecommerce eligibility.

expected_actions.name: Meta - Event - Purchase
expected_actions.justification: Explicit media brief and current official browser schema.

external_dependencies: Meta catalog and optimization setup remain platform-side dependencies.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to configure Meta AddToCart in an authorized dedicated workspace where the approved catalog contract requires every item to have a non-empty merchant SKU. The installed template does not suppress firing when a transform returns an empty value. Runtime recette and publication are outside this request.

expected_actions.name: CJS - Meta items valid
expected_actions.justification: Narrow deterministic guard checks every required merchant SKU.

expected_actions.name: CE - add_to_cart - Meta valid
expected_actions.justification: The event is eligible only when all required items are valid.

expected_actions.name: Meta - Event - AddToCart
expected_actions.justification: Explicit media brief and current official browser schema.

external_dependencies: The site must continue to provide merchant SKU values in the approved item path.
expected_invariants: invalid required item prevents tag eligibility
zero valid numeric values are preserved
empty transform is not treated as a firing gate
forbidden_invariants: invalid item filtered out while tag fires
analytics item_id assumed to be catalog ID

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement strict Meta gating in an authorized dedicated workspace where the approved CMP contract requires both advertising-purpose grant and the exact Meta vendor grant through separate documented native variables. Runtime recette and publication are outside this request.

expected_actions.name: Block - CMP - advertising purpose denied
expected_actions.justification: Independent required purpose grant needs OR-denial behavior.

expected_actions.name: Block - CMP - Meta denied
expected_actions.justification: Independent required vendor grant needs OR-denial behavior.

expected_actions.name: Meta browser tags
expected_actions.justification: Every consumer must carry both independent denial blocks.

external_dependencies: CMP must continue to expose the approved documented states.
expected_invariants: both blocks attach to every Meta consumer
forbidden_invariants: one trigger with mutually exclusive AND denials

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement explicitly approved advanced Consent Mode for Google Ads only in an authorized dedicated workspace. Current official product documentation, the CMP template, default/update behavior, denied-state ping policy, and a supported destination separation from basic-blocked GA4 are proven. Runtime recette and publication are outside this request.

expected_actions.name: Consent Initialization - CMP
expected_actions.justification: Explicit approved default and update signal behavior.

expected_actions.name: Google Ads advanced consent execution unit
expected_actions.justification: Officially supported separated Ads route must remain eligible for approved denied-state behavior.

expected_actions.name: Block - CMP - GA4 denied
expected_actions.justification: GA4 remains on the separate basic route.

external_dependencies: The approved CMP and Google product policies remain client-owned.
expected_invariants: Google Ads block does not defeat approved pings
forbidden_invariants: advanced mode enabled from vendor name alone

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement GA4 basic blocking and Google Ads advanced Consent Mode on one currently shared Google tag, but no officially supported destination separation has been supplied.
external_dependencies: The analyst must approve a current officially supported execution-unit separation or compatible consent policy.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement generate_lead where the approved requirement names the business action but supplies no success moment, dataLayer event, field types, or representative payload and these facts cannot be discovered safely.
external_dependencies: Approved source event, success timing, field contract, and representative payload are required.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved GA4 sign_up event with a complete source contract, but no MCP, API, authorized import, or signed-in UI mutation path is available.
external_dependencies: An authorized GTM mutation and saved-readback path is required.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm in an authorized dedicated workspace. A constant and DLV save successfully, then the approved community-template tag write fails with an uncertain response that saved readback cannot resolve.
scenarios.recovery_boundary: Stop the failed tag and all dependent writes; preserve unrelated and successfully saved current-operation objects pending an authorized recovery decision.

expected_actions.name: CST - vendor pixel_id
expected_actions.justification: Approved stable destination identity.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.value
expected_actions.justification: Approved source field.

external_dependencies: Resolve the uncertain template write before dependent mutation.
expected_invariants: exact saved IDs and fingerprints recorded

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm after a prior authorized implementation where every saved object, reference, template field, consent setting, and current fingerprint already matches the approved configuration map.

expected_actions.name: Existing approved implementation
expected_actions.justification: Every semantic dimension and saved field matches.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved GA4 custom event and custom parameter in an authorized dedicated workspace; the analyst also requests reporting registration and key-event designation outside GTM.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - custom_event
expected_actions.justification: Approved analytics event and exact parameter set.

external_dependencies: Register the GA4 custom definition and designate the key event outside GTM.
expected_invariants: GTM collection separated from GA4 administration

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an unlisted browser media vendor in an authorized dedicated workspace with an explicit media brief, complete source contract, current official browser event and consent documentation, and an inspected installed template.

expected_actions.name: Official vendor browser implementation
expected_actions.justification: Generic media mapping contract is established by current official and template evidence.

external_dependencies: Platform-side conversion or optimization setup is recorded separately.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved technically valid GA4 custom event account_created in an authorized dedicated workspace. Current official documentation identifies sign_up as potentially more appropriate, and the approved source contract remains unchanged.

discrepancies.default_action: Report the advisory and preserve account_created.

expected_actions.name: CE - account_created
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved source event.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - account_created
expected_actions.justification: Exact technically valid approved analytics event.

external_dependencies: The tracking-plan owner may separately approve a future measurement-plan revision.
forbidden_invariants: tracking plan optimized by configuration skill

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved analytics row whose requested manual event is established by current official documentation as reserved and unsupported, with no amended analytics decision.
external_dependencies: The tracking-plan owner must supply an amended approved analytics decision.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved event containing exactly one approved parameter in an authorized dedicated workspace. Current official documentation recommends another compatible but optional field.

discrepancies.default_action: Keep the approved field set unchanged.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - approved_custom_event
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved event and parameter set.

external_dependencies: Any parameter addition requires a new explicit analytics decision.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved workbook scope containing one visible event sheet, one explicitly approved hidden event sheet, one reference dictionary, and one explicitly excluded legacy sheet in an authorized dedicated workspace.

expected_actions.name: Two approved event implementations
expected_actions.justification: The explicit source-scope manifest includes both event sheets.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved event in an authorized dedicated workspace where the source value is directly available through a DLV but the existing container commonly uses broad Custom JavaScript helpers for similar values.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.plan_type
expected_actions.justification: The best-practice direct mapping is selected before local reuse.

expected_actions.name: Existing broad Custom JavaScript helper
expected_actions.justification: It is nonconformant and outside cleanup scope.

external_dependencies: Existing unrelated helper debt belongs to a separate cleanup review.
forbidden_invariants: container prevalence treated as best practice

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement an approved event when a confirmed existing automatic implementation would duplicate it, but updating or disabling that existing implementation is not authorized.
external_dependencies: The analyst must authorize reconciliation of the conflicting existing implementation.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm through an authorized adapter whose current schema exposes product-specific action names, a maximum page size of twenty for one object family, cursor pagination, batch readback limits, and working mutation access.

expected_actions.name: Existing exact source trigger
expected_actions.justification: All relevant pages prove semantic equivalence.

expected_actions.name: Approved destination event
expected_actions.justification: Exact discovered adapter action and fields can save the approved configuration.

external_dependencies: Adapter credentials and quota must remain valid through readback.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm in an authorized dedicated workspace containing two confirmed pre-existing changes. The current operation updates one approved tag, saved readback passes, and the identical rerun is a no-op.

expected_actions.name: Approved analytics event
expected_actions.justification: The current operation changes only the approved tag.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.
expected_invariants: final total not attributed to current run

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement three approved hostname-to-destination mappings for one vendor in an authorized dedicated workspace. The mappings share event schema and consent policy, have a safe no-match requirement, and create several related objects.

expected_actions.name: Media - Vendor campaign
expected_actions.justification: Several related current-operation objects benefit from shallow grouping.

expected_actions.name: LUT - vendor destination by hostname
expected_actions.justification: Three real deterministic mappings share schema and consent with a safe no-match result.

expected_actions.name: Vendor - Event - Conversion
expected_actions.justification: One readable consumer uses the approved destination lookup.

external_dependencies: Destination identities remain owned by the media team.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to implement a required browser media event, but the installed template version cannot represent one current officially required field and no authorized compatible template update or alternative implementation is available.
external_dependencies: Authorize a compatible installed template version or supported implementation path.
expected_invariants: required field retained in intended contract

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to add a server-side GTM route, browser/server event IDs, and CAPI deduplication to an otherwise approved client-side analytics and media design.
external_dependencies: A future authorized server-side and deduplication capability must own this requirement.
